channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: TSMC : le procédé 2 nm s’annonce comme un écrasant succès commercial, pourquoi c’est une mauvaise nouvelle pour Apple
item.description: Le procédé 2 nm de TSMC n'est pas encore exploité à grande échelle, mais il affiche déjà tous les signes précurseurs d'un immense succès commercial. Une perspective délectable pour le fondeur taïwanais, dont le retour sur investissement s'annonce substantiel, mais cette prospérité attendue semble aussi de bien mauvais augure pour Apple.

item.title: 'I thought it was satire': Sam Altman mocks Anthropic's latest AI ad that's getting widely blasted for being 'dystopian marketing slop'
item.description: There's a new Anthropic ad for Claude in the wild, but it hasn't had quite the impact that its creators might have been hoping for.

item.title: Essential Gear for an Emergency Kit—for Cars or Go-Bags
item.description: We consulted preparedness experts and WIRED’s team of testers for the essential gear to keep on hand in case of wildfires, earthquakes, and lord knows what else.

item.title: Viaim OpenNote review: I wore this open-ear voice recorder all day, and its AI meeting summaries proved invaluable
item.description: Open-ear design is more comfortable and social than standard earphones, but music is secondary to the AI feature set.

item.title: 5 Best Smart Speakers (2026): Alexa, Google, Apple
item.description: Looking to add a smart speaker to your house? Here’s which to choose, whether you’re an Alexa, Siri, or Gemini fan.

item.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced director says the team scrapped the original hidden blade combat because it 'prioritized core combat' but is 'listening to the community' and is 'interested in what people want the most'
item.description: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced director Richard Knight has explained why the remaster doesn't feature the original game's hidden blade combat.

item.title: Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal
item.description: A $400 million chip-backed loan points to the next wave of AI infrastructure deals.

item.title: Trust is borrowed, not owned
item.description: Leaders should act accordingly. When Spirit Airlines collapsed in May, its travelers weren’t the only stakeholders left stranded. Thousands of employees lost their jobs overnight, with some filing a lawsuit over owed pay and benefits and others turning to GoFundMe for emergency financial support.

item.title: Designing for the edges improves the center
item.description: When products work for people with the most constraints, they tend to work better for everyone. In 1990, the Americans with Disabilities Act mandated curb cuts at intersections across the country. The requirement was specific: Wheelchair users needed a ramp from sidewalk to street. What happened next was probably unexpected. Cyclists used the cuts. Parents with strollers used them. Delivery workers with hand trucks. Travelers with rolling luggage. A design solution created for a specific need ultimately became the standard for everyone, because it addressed a real human constraint instead of a...

item.title: Test de la Redmagic Astra 2 : la tablette gaming compacte qui refroidit à l’eau
item.description: Un an après une première Astra convaincante, Redmagic remet le couvert avec une tablette de 9 pouces encore plus musclée, dotée d'un refroidissement liquide visible… et audible.

item.title: iPhone fans aren't happy as Apple closes a popular money-saving loophole — but there’s better news for iPad buyers
item.description: Apple will no longer let you buy an unlocked iPhone on carrier financing direct from its website.

item.title: Military personnel will be tested for low testosterone. Is that a good idea?
item.description: Under Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s new screening program, testosterone replacement therapy will be suggested to service members who are deficient. A new mandatory screening program will test all U.S. service members for testosterone deficiency in an initiative to optimize soldiers’ performance and their long-term health.

item.title: GameStop CEO says Sony's axing of physical games 'doesn't matter at all' in a statement sure to enrage gamers
item.description: GameStop CEO shares hot take on Sony's decision to eliminate physical game copies on PlayStation, one that's only set to harm consumers rather than help.

item.title: This clever Anker portable projector is 19% off at Amazon — and its flippable speakers are a win for presentations and screenings
item.description: Save big on the Anker Nebula P1i projector with 380 ANSI lumens, 4K support, and built-in Google TV.

item.title: Chez Tesla, la batterie la plus endurante n’est pas celle que vous croyez
item.description: Une nouvelle analyse portant sur près de 10 000 tests réels de batteries de voitures électriques montre qu'une même Tesla Model 3 vieillit très différemment selon la batterie qui se cache sous son plancher, et que c'est justement la version la moins chère qui tient le mieux la distance.

item.title: What is the Codex Micro? OpenAI's first hardware gadget explained
item.description: Confused about what the point of the Codex Micro is? Here's everything you need to know, including how it's been received by the target audience of coders.

item.title: The Medion Signium 27 S1 isn't going to turn the computing world upside down, but it's a surprisingly capable all-in-one desktop with a sensible price tag
item.description: The Medion Signium 27 S1 is a capable and reasonably priced all-in-one desktop PC, but it hardly sparks excitement and the bundled peripherals are weak.

item.title: Tesla's selling a $225 balance bike for toddlers
item.description: Tesla's Autopilot may fail to recognize children at times, but the company certainly recognizes their potential as future buyers.

item.title: Teenage TfL hackers sentenced to years in prison following Scattered Spider attacks
item.description: Two young men pleaded guilty to hacking into Transport for London in 2024 and were given long prison sentences.

item.title: C’est un véritable déstockage : la manette Backbone One Lightning PS4/PS5 est à -87 %
item.description: La Backbone One Lightning est une manette mobile pouvant accueillir un iPhone pour jouer en Remote Play à votre console PS4 ou PS5, vendue 14,99 euros sur Micromania-Zing et 119,99 euros sur le site officiel.

item.title: This sneaky Lenovo promo code combo cuts almost $400 off the ThinkPad T14 Gen 7 AMD — and it works on other PCs, too
item.description: With an AMD Ryzen 5 PRO processor, 16GB of RAM, and two stackable discounts, Lenovo's ThinkPad T14 Gen 7 AMD is an easy recommendation from me.

item.title: Forget AI training data. This startup learned from slime mold
item.description: Mireta’s software taps into slime mold’s natural ability for urban planning. In a classic experiment more than a decade ago, researchers in Japan gave a slime mold—a single-celled organism known for forming efficient networks—a “map” of the cities around Tokyo. They represented each municipality with an oat flake, the organism’s favorite food, and watched as it created a network that looked eerily similar to the Japanese rail system....

item.title: Human-led, AI-assisted testing: Why AI won’t replace penetration testers...yet.
item.description: Why human expertise remains essential in AI-powered testing.

item.title: Valve Steam Machine Review: A Compromised Console
item.description: Great for indie darlings, but Sony, Microsoft, and Nintendo remain kings of TV gaming.

item.title: The war on ‘woke science’ comes for space research
item.description: The Trump administration is waging a culture war on science, and the latest salvo is in the form of a dry, bureaucratic proposal from the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) that could threaten the future of US science as we know it. The proposal would give political appointees unprecedented control over grant funding, the […]

item.title: How to watch Belgian Grand Prix 2026 F1: live stream from anywhere
item.description: All the ways to watch Belgian Grand Prix 2026 live streams online and from anywhere, and potentially for free.

item.title: J’ai testé un smartphone à 38 °C : la chaleur détruit-elle votre batterie ?
item.description: Un smartphone au soleil consomme-t-il autant que dans une cave bien fraîche ? C'est la question à laquelle on répond aujourd'hui et on vous dit si c'est dangereux.

item.title: You can't deny it any more: physical music is back in a big way with new sales figures proving it... and no, this time it's not vinyl
item.description: Stats prove that nostalgic CD-buying is even bigger than nostalgic vinyl-buying, which could prompt a sea change for some music lovers.

item.title: Google Agenda pourrait bientôt corriger un oubli majeur sur nos smartphones
item.description: Google Agenda va enfin permettre de créer des sessions de « temps de concentration » directement depuis votre smartphone.

item.title: Signal is testing the ability to add an Android phone or tablet as a secondary device
item.description: Signal's latest beta comes with a feature that will make it easier to use the app on multiple devices.

item.title: La fin des soldes approche ? Boulanger prend déjà la relève et lance une grande braderie
item.description: Alors que les soldes se terminent d'ici quelques jours, Boulanger prend déjà la relève ! L'enseigne enchaîne directement avec une grande braderie et casse les prix sur une large sélection Tech (smartphones, TV, audio…) jusqu'au 11 août. Pas de temps mort pour les bonnes affaires : découvrez notre sélection ultime avant l'épuisement des stocks !

item.title: Windows 10 still powers one in six PCs around today — and that could be a major security issue very soon
item.description: The average Windows 10 device has around 3x as many active CVEs as a Windows 11 device, and migration is slowing down.

item.title: Panasonic Japanese Microwave Review (2026): True 1-Button Cooking
item.description: The Japanese Microwave measures food temperature directly, and stops cooking when food’s hot. It’s kinda terrific.

item.title: 23 Best Gifts for Cooks (2026): Vitamix, Frying Pans, Air Fryers
item.description: From the best air fryer to frying pans to knife sharpeners anyone can use, these ideas will keep the curious home chef in your life tinkering away.

item.title: How to watch Ann Droid online from anywhere – it's *FREE*
item.description: Here's how to watch Ann Droid, a new British comedy series starring Diane Morgan, online from anywhere in the world, completely FREE.

item.title: 'The first week of pre-orders brought in $180 million' — Analysts report that GTA 6 has 'the strongest pre-order campaign ever recorded'
item.description: Grand Theft Auto 6 reportedly achieved the strongest pre-order period ever and is on track to earn over $5 billion by the end of its launch week.

item.title: A $10K Bounty Aims to Make Sony’s PlayStation 5 a Computer Again
item.description: Nice PlayStation you got. Bet it could probably run Linux too.

item.title: Score $12 off the Kodak Step Instant Smartphone Photo Printer at Amazon
item.description: As of July 17, the Kodak Step Instant Smartphone Photo Printer is discounted to $67.99 at Amazon, 15% off its list price of $79.99.

item.title: Nothing Phone (3) gets another price cut at Amazon — is this under-rated phone now a better buy than the Pixel 10, Galaxy S25, or iPhone 17?
item.description: We're big fans of the Nothing Phone (3) at TechRadar — especially when it's going this cheap at Amazon.

item.title: Who decides what runs on your website?
item.description: AI in hosting works best when customers choose the on-switch.

item.title: Samsung casse le prix de son TV Micro RGB de 65 pouces : 1 311 € au lieu de 2 399 €
item.description: Le Samsung TMR65R85H est l'un des premiers téléviseurs Micro RGB du marché, une technologie qui promet un bond en qualité d'image par rapport aux Neo QLED. Ce modèle 65 pouces est affiché à 2 399 euros, mais grâce à une ODR et un code promo, il tombe à 1 311 euros sur Ubaldi.

item.title: A unified front against fraud: Securing the UK's payments future
item.description: As sophisticated scams escalate, can a unified front finally secure the UK’s payments?

item.title: Google ajoute un espace pour vos propres infos dans Google Contacts
item.description: Fini les trous de mémoire et autres recherches interminables pour retrouver son numéro de téléphone. Google commence enfin à déployer la section « Vos informations » dans son application Contacts, un moyen simple de retrouver et gérer ses coordonnées personnelles.

item.title: God Of War TV series is recasting Kratos
item.description: Amazon's upcoming God Of War show has hit a major snag - it's now on the hunt for a new Kratos, after an on-set injury put its current lead actor out of commission. Sons of Anarchy star Ryan Hurst was originally cast for the role in January, with Deadline reporting that four episodes of the […]
